purpose: Translate legacy/theoretical terms into clearer Operator-Intelligence training terms, with strict meaning preserved.
author: Phan Thành Trung (Trung Bờm)

notes: Translations must not change the strict meaning.
When a term is ambiguous, return a warning rather than force a wrong mapping.

concepts.definition_strict: A property, law, or quantity that remains unchanged under a specified set/group of admissible transformations.
concepts.definition_plain: Dù bạn đổi cách nhìn/biểu diễn (trong phạm vi phép biến đổi hợp lệ), luật nền vẫn giữ nguyên.

disambiguation.handling: Do NOT force-translate to invariance unless the text explicitly refers to invariance under transformations. If purely about shapes, keep 'geometric symmetry'.

anti_patterns.why_wrong: Symmetry in physics/math refers to invariance under transformations; aesthetic symmetry is only one special case.

examples.input: When the system dissolves back to symmetry, it returns to the base law.
examples.output: When the system dissolves back to invariance (bất biến), it returns to the base law.

examples.input: Symmetry is the background rule that survives all valid transformations.
examples.output: Invariance (bất biến) is the background rule that survives all valid transformations.

examples.input: This crystal has mirror symmetry along one axis.
examples.output: This crystal has geometric symmetry along one axis.
examples.note: Disambiguation: geometric context → keep geometric symmetry.

tests.name: physics_symmetry_to_invariance
tests.input: symmetry of the Hamiltonian under rotations

concepts.new_term_vi: tầng nền bất khả phân rã
concepts.definition_strict: A condition indicating no further meaningful decomposition exists; the object/operator has reached an irreducible base level where additional splitting yields no simpler valid structure.
concepts.definition_plain: Đã gọt/cắt tới mức không thể chia nhỏ thêm mà vẫn giữ ý nghĩa; phần còn lại là nền (axiom/base).

disambiguation.handling: Height=0 is not 'easy'; it means 'no remaining reducible structure'. Translate as 'irreducible base layer' and optionally add note.

mapping_rules.replacement: height = 0 (irreducible base layer / tầng nền bất khả phân rã)
h = 0 (irreducible base layer / tầng nền bất khả phân rã)

anti_patterns.why_wrong: Height=0 means irreducible: there is no smaller valid decomposition step left, not that the task was easy.

examples.input: At height=0, the operator cannot be decomposed further.
examples.output: At height = 0 (irreducible base layer / tầng nền bất khả phân rã), the operator cannot be decomposed further.

examples.input: Brauer height = 0 implies the remaining structure is the base law.
examples.output: height = 0 (irreducible base layer / tầng nền bất khả phân rã) implies the remaining structure is the base law.
This is not 'easy'; height = 0 (irreducible base layer / tầng nền bất khả phân rã) means no further valid decomposition remains.
